description: Intersection cross-traffic emergency: ego has a close follower, a truck crosses from the left on a constructed collision course, and the nominal right-side escape path is occupied by a pedestrian and blocked by roadside clutter. Sweep sub-rule-boundary TTC, ego speed, and right-side clearance to test the blind spot where existing left-crossing/right-escape rules exclude the pedestrian/blocked corridor or only cover Normal Road at TTC >= 1.4 s.
hypothesis: With the right escape corridor blocked by a pedestrian at short TTC, the dominant maneuver should switch from right-drift escape to hard in-lane braking or left-behind-the-truck steering, with the boundary driven mainly by TTC, ego speed, and remaining right-side clearance.
